faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.metaDescription: Find answers to common questions about the #BuildingResilience conference — April 17, 2026, Libreville, Gabon.
faq.intro: Everything you need to know about the conference

whatIs.question: What is #BuildingResilience?
whatIs.answer: #BuildingResilience is a pan-African campaign led by the First Lady of Gabon, Her Excellency Mrs. Zita Oligui Nguema, focused on strengthening resilience for women and girls facing climate change and conflict across the continent.

attending.question: Who is attending?
attending.answer: The conference brings together 13 African First Ladies, alongside delegates, partners, and civil society organisations committed to the four pillars of CAP 241.

link.label: View the full list of First Ladies

when.question: When and where is the event?
when.answer: The conference takes place on April 17, 2026, in Libreville, Gabon.

pillars.question: What are the four pillars of CAP 241?
pillars.answer: The four pillars are Health, Women, Youth, and Education — the foundational areas through which the campaign strengthens resilience for communities across Africa.

oaflad.question: What is OAFLAD?
oaflad.answer: OAFLAD (Organization of African First Ladies for Development) is a continental body that brings together African First Ladies to advocate for health, education, and women's empowerment across the continent.

follow.question: How can I follow the event?
follow.answer: Follow #BuildingResilience on social media: @resilience241 on X/Twitter, @resilience_241 on Instagram, and on Facebook. Live updates will be shared throughout the conference.

balance.question: What is the BALANCE programme?
balance.answer: BALANCE (ÉQUILIBRES 2026–2029) is a multi-year programme under CAP 241 that coordinates action across three axes: Prevent, Support, and Rebuild — addressing the root causes and consequences of fragility for women and girls.

link.label: Learn more about the BALANCE programme

about.metaDescription: About the #BuildingResilience campaign led by OAFLAD — strengthening resilience for women and girls facing climate change and conflict across Africa.
about.title: About
about.campaignTitle: An African Initiative to Strengthen Resilience
about.campaignIntro: The continental campaign #BuildingResilience, led by the Organization of African First Ladies for Development (OAFLAD), reflects a collective effort to address the growing vulnerabilities facing African societies.
about.campaignWomen: women and girls emerge as both the most exposed and the central drivers of resilience within families and communities.
about.campaignConviction: This continental initiative is grounded in a strong conviction:
about.campaignConvictionText: resilience cannot be sustainable unless it is structured, supported, and collectively driven.
about.campaignGoalsIntro: Through this campaign, OAFLAD calls for:

campaignGoals.systems: Strengthening social and health protection systems
campaignGoals.women: Supporting women in their central role within families and communities
campaignGoals.responses: Developing coordinated responses tailored to local realities

about.campaignFramework: The #BuildingResilience campaign therefore provides a continental framework for mobilization, fostering the exchange of experiences, coordination of actions, and the development of sustainable solutions.
about.gabonIntro: Faced with the challenges of our time, Gabon has made a clear choice:
about.gabonChoice: To contribute to the continental #BuildingResilience initiative by placing health and family stability at the core of its commitment.
about.gabonConviction: This choice is based on a simple conviction:
about.gabonConvictionText: Resilience cannot be declared. It must be built.
about.gabonBuilt: It is built through contact with reality, by listening to those who carry the weight of vulnerability every day.
about.gabonProgramme: The BALANCE Programme 2026–2029, part of the national CAP 241 framework, represents Gabon's contribution to this African initiative.

gabonCommitments.national: To structure a coherent national response
gabonCommitments.root: To address the root causes of vulnerability

about.gabonHealth: Health is not a sectoral policy.
about.gabonStability: It is a condition for stability.
about.gabonLever: It is a lever for sustainable resilience.
about.terrainTour: Before the conference, the First Lady chose to go and meet women directly. From April 7 to 13, she visited 18 neighbourhoods, including the most vulnerable, in Libreville, Akanda, Owendo and Ntoum.
about.terrainApproach: An approach deliberately rooted in working-class neighbourhoods, as close as possible to everyday realities.
about.terrainNarrative: On the ground, women spoke up to share their daily realities, their difficulties accessing healthcare, but also their expectations and their hopes.
about.terrainQuote1: We start treatments, but we can't always see them through.
about.terrainAxesIntro: From these encounters, three major challenges emerged:
about.zonQuote: On this day of April 17, National Women's Day in Gabon, I wish to address each and every one of you. To those present here. To those working in our cities, in our villages, in our homes. And to those whose commitment, often unseen, continues to sustain our society.
about.zonQuote2: Because behind the balances we observe, there is, very often, the silent burden carried by women. When fragilities emerge — social, economic or health-related — they are the ones who absorb, organize, and sustain. But the resilience of a Nation cannot rely solely on this silent strength. It must be structured. It must be supported. It must be organized.
about.zonQuote3: Through the BALANCE Programme 2026–2029, which I officially launch today, we choose to act on what sustainably underpins stability: continuity of care, recognition of vulnerabilities, and the rebuilding of life trajectories.
about.zonAuthor: Her Excellency Mrs Zita Oligui Nguema
about.fondationIntro: The Ma Banniere Foundation  is guided by a clear vision:
about.fondationVision: to contribute to social stability by delivering structured responses to human, health, and community vulnerabilities.
about.fondationCatalyst: It acts as a catalyst for solutions, mobilizing high-impact initiatives in health, women's empowerment, youth development, and community resilience.
about.fondationOaflad: Within the framework of the continental dynamic led by OAFLAD, the Foundation contributes to the implementation of structured initiatives at the national level.
about.fondationPrinciplesIntro: Its mission is based on three key principles:
about.fondationKeyMessage: The Foundation acts as a catalyst for social, health, and community solutions.
about.closingQuote: Under Ma Banniere, we care, we share, we protect.
about.closingCta: Discover the structural framework behind this vision.

cap241.metaDescription: CAP 241 — Gabon's strategic framework for social action under the #BuildingResilience campaign, linking health, family stability, prevention, and skills transfer.
cap241.title: CAP 241 – Zita Oligui Nguema's strategic framework
cap241.heroTitle: CAP 241: A Framework for Social Action
cap241.heroIntro: The work of the <link>Ma Banniere Foundation</link> is structured through CAP 241, a framework linking:
cap241.pillarsIntro: These pillars do not function in isolation. They operate in a logic of interdependence:
cap241.pillarsArchitecture: This architecture avoids fragmentation of interventions. It fosters a cross-cutting, coherent and sustainable approach.

pillar1.name: CAP Health
pillar1.description: Health is the foundation of stability. When care pathways break, life trajectories collapse.

pillar2.name: CAP Women
pillar2.description: Supporting women in their central role within families and communities through skills transfer and empowerment.

pillar3.name: CAP Youth
pillar3.description: Rebuilding life paths for young people through training, mentoring and reintegration pathways.

pillar4.name: CAP Education
pillar4.description: Strengthening educational foundations to build lasting resilience through prevention and awareness.

cap241.balanceIntro: Women often carry the weight of fragility. BALANCE provides structured responses:
cap241.balanceAmbition: A clear ambition: to transform fragilities into stabilized trajectories. To move from silence to support. To build sustainable responses.
cap241.nkokIntro: In response to the identified vulnerabilities, action cannot be fragmented or temporary. It must be structured, coherent, and sustained over time.
cap241.nkokCenter: It is within this perspective that the Nkok Integrated Center for Rehabilitation, Training, and Reintegration is being developed.
cap241.nkokDescription: This center represents a concrete response to fragile life trajectories by providing:

nkokServices.reception: Tailored reception and support for vulnerable individuals
nkokServices.followup: Continuous follow-up aimed at rebuilding sustainable life paths

cap241.nkokApproach: Beyond its operational function, the center embodies a comprehensive approach:
cap241.nkokAmbition: This initiative reflects a clear ambition: to move from silent vulnerability to structured, visible, and sustainable responses.

programme.panelsIntro: The discussions are designed to deepen the issues related to social and health resilience. They will allow sharing experiences, identifying common challenges, and developing tailored solutions.

panelists.name: Mrs Micheline Otounga
panelists.role: Former Advisor to the Prime Minister on social affairs. Specialist in women's rights.

panelists.name: Mrs Odile Boungat Ossawa
panelists.role: Clinical Psychologist. Former Head of the Gender Department at the Prime Minister's Office. Expert in gender, GBV and reproductive health.

panelists.name: Dr Ida Flore Maroundou
panelists.role: President of AURORE NGO. Director of the 'A Refuge for Her' Centre for women victims of violence.

panelists.name: Mrs Reine Ambourouet Ogandaga
panelists.role: Director, National Centre for Mental Health.

panel2.title: Panel 2
panel2.topic: Women's Empowerment: a Public Governance Challenge

panelists.role: Specialist, Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock.

panelists.name: Mrs Inès Boussougou
panelists.role: Director General for Social Economy, Ministry of Commerce and SMEs.

panelists.name: Mrs Bouanga
panelists.role: Expert representative of the Ministry of Planning, specialist in good governance.

panelists.name: Mr Hugues Mbadinga
panelists.role: Director General for the Promotion of Women and Gender.

speakers.metaDescription: Patronage of the OAFLAD #BuildingResilience conference — distinguished leaders and First Ladies championing resilience across Africa.
speakers.title: Patronage & Dignitaries
speakers.intro: The #BuildingResilience conference is placed under the High Patronage of the President of the Republic of Gabon, His Excellency Mr Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema.
speakers.content: Moderator profiles will be announced soon.

president.name: His Excellency Mr Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema
president.title: President of the Gabonese Republic
president.quote:
Building a new foundation means choosing a society that is more just, more balanced, and firmly centered on people.

In this vision, valuing human capital is essential. It requires a clear commitment to education, social protection, and inclusion — particularly for women and young people.

Because there can be no sustainable development without equity. There can be no strong nation without the full participation of women.

Gabonese women are at the heart of this dynamic. Every day, they play a vital role in maintaining social balance, passing on values, and driving collective progress.

The Gabon we are building is one where every woman finds her place, moves forward with dignity, and where no one is left behind.

speakers.partnersClosingText: See the partners who have helped to make this possible.

saoTome.name: Maria de Fátima Vila Nova
saoTome.country: Democratic Republic of São Tomé and Príncipe

legal.privacyIntro: The Organisation of African First Ladies for Development (OAFLAD) is committed to protecting your personal data. This policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard information provided through the resilience241.com website.
legal.dataCollected: When you register for the #BuildingResilience conference, we collect the following information: first name, last name, email address, phone number, organisation, role, category, and preferred language.
legal.purpose: Your personal data is used exclusively by OAFLAD for the purpose of organising and administering the #BuildingResilience conference. This includes managing registrations, communicating event details, and coordinating logistics. Your data is not shared with, sold to, or disclosed to any third parties.
legal.retention: Your personal data is retained for the duration necessary to organise the conference and for a reasonable period thereafter for administrative purposes. You may request the deletion of your data at any time by contacting us.
legal.rights: You have the right to access, correct, or request the deletion of your personal data. To exercise these rights, please contact us at the address below.
legal.security: We implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your personal data against unauthorised access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction.
legal.contactText: For any questions regarding this privacy policy or your personal data, please contact the OAFLAD organising team via the <contactLink>contact page</contactLink>.
legal.termsIntro: By accessing and using the resilience241.com website, you agree to the following terms and conditions.
legal.use: This website is operated by OAFLAD to provide information about the #BuildingResilience campaign and conference. You may use this website for personal, non-commercial purposes only. You agree not to misuse the website or its content.
legal.ip: All content on this website, including text, images, logos, and design elements, is the property of OAFLAD or its partners and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You may not reproduce, distribute, or modify any content without prior written permission.
legal.registration: By submitting a registration through this website, you confirm that the information provided is accurate and complete. OAFLAD reserves the right to refuse or cancel registrations at its discretion.
legal.liability: OAFLAD makes every effort to ensure the accuracy of information on this website. However, we do not guarantee that the content is error-free or uninterrupted. OAFLAD shall not be liable for any direct or indirect damages arising from the use of this website.
legal.changes: OAFLAD reserves the right to update these terms at any time. Continued use of the website constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
legal.termsContactText: For any questions regarding these terms, please contact the OAFLAD organising team via the <contactLink>contact page</contactLink>.
